title: Three-Color DFS Cycle Detection
description: Interactive vis-network visualization demonstrating the three-color DFS algorithm for detecting cycles in directed graphs, specifically for validating learning graph DAG structure
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: algorithms
graph theory
cycle detection
depth-first search
learning graphs
DAG validation

controls.navigation_buttons: Zoom and pan the network view
controls.legend: Shows the three colors (White, Gray, Black) and their meanings
controls.stack_display: Shows the current DFS traversal stack
controls.cycle_alert: Highlights when a cycle is detected with explanation

learningObjectives: Explain the meaning of the three node colors in DFS cycle detection: white (unvisited), gray (on the current traversal stack), and black (fully explored)
Trace a depth-first search through a directed graph while nodes change color and the stack display grows and shrinks
Predict that a cycle exists when the traversal reaches a gray node that is already on the DFS stack
Explain why a learning graph must be a directed acyclic graph and how three-color DFS validates that no prerequisite cycles exist
